retrieved_context: Validation rule for entity: Row-level hierarchy match. Check type row_level_match. Description: Normalized hierarchy rows should match exactly between source and target.. Business rationale: Entity hierarchy reconciliation should stay transparent and explainable for business users and data engineering..

Validation rule for entity: Mapping completeness. Check type mapping_completeness. Description: Every source member must have a valid mapping record before migration is accepted.. Business rationale: Entity hierarchy reconciliation should stay transparent and explainable for business users and data engineering..

Validation rule for entity: Level consistency. Check type level_consistency. Description: Hierarchy levels must remain consistent after normalization.. Business rationale: Entity hierarchy reconciliation should stay transparent and explainable for business users and data engineering..

Validation rule for entity: Source parent vs target parent mismatch. Check type parent_mismatch. Description: Target parent-child relationships must match the Smart View source extract.. Business rationale: Entity hierarchy reconciliation should stay transparent and explainable for business users and data engineering..
results.likely_cause: Numeric measure data differs between source and target, which usually points to an aggregation, currency, or row-level load mismatch.
results.recommended_action: Compare source and target measure rows on the shared business keys, then reconcile any mismatched numeric values or missing measure rows.

agent_explanation:
### What Was Tested
- Entity - Missing members in target, Entity - Parent existence, Entity - Source parent vs target parent mismatch, Entity - Duplicate members, Entity - Leaf/non-leaf consistency, Entity - Level consistency, Entity - Row-level hierarchy match, Entity - Numeric value match

### What Failed
- Entity - Numeric value match (11 failed records)
- 1 failed checks were recorded and 11 records were flagged.

### Likely Cause
- Numeric measure data differs between source and target, which usually points to an aggregation, currency, or row-level load mismatch.

### Recommended Action
- Compare source and target measure rows on the shared business keys, then reconcile any mismatched numeric values or missing measure rows.
